title: utilities.js — checkWhiteSpace hardened with explicit null/undefined guard
commit_message: fix: prevent checkWhiteSpace from crashing on null/undefined input

why_micro_hotfix.explanation: Only one symbol changed in one file. The change is a defensive null guard — not a behaviour change for valid inputs. Yet the ripple is measurable: 2 CRITICAL (direct tests), 3 more CRITICAL (capitalizeFirstLetter depends on checkWhiteSpace and has checkWhiteSpace in its symbols[]), and 12 HIGH (sibling utilities + cross-dependent tests that list utilities.js as a source).
why_micro_hotfix.key_insight: A 3-line hotfix generates 5 CRITICAL + 12 HIGH = 17 selected tests. This is the minimal realistic selection — enough to confirm no regressions without running all 77.

CRITICAL.reason: describe() label = 'checkWhiteSpace' (direct match) OR symbols[] contains 'checkWhiteSpace' (indirect match via capitalizeFirstLetter)

tests.test: checkWhiteSpace > returns true for a string that contains a space
checkWhiteSpace > returns false for a string with no whitespace
capitalizeFirstLetter with checkWhiteSpace > capitalizes the first letter of each word in a multi-word string
tests.why: describe = 'capitalizeFirstLetter with checkWhiteSpace'; symbols includes 'checkWhiteSpace' — changed symbol; sources = [utilities.js]

tests.test: capitalizeFirstLetter with checkWhiteSpace > capitalizes a single word correctly without whitespace branch
capitalizeFirstLetter with checkWhiteSpace > returns undefined when given a non-string truthy value after type guard fix

HIGH.reason: Same source file (utilities.js) changed — sibling symbols selected defensively

tests.test: checkNull > returns an empty string when the value is null
tests.why: sources = [utilities.js]; sibling symbol in same file

tests.test: checkNull > returns the original value when it is defined and non-null
checkArray > returns an empty array when the input is null
checkArray > returns the original array when it contains items
getTimes > correctly parses 3661 seconds into 1h 01m 01s
getTimes > returns 0 hours when the duration is under 3600 seconds
getProgressWidth > calculates 50% when value is half of max
getProgressWidth > returns 0 when the value argument is missing or falsy
isUserLoggedIn reads storageKeys.token > returns false when no token is stored
isUserLoggedIn reads storageKeys.token > returns true after a token has been stored under storageKeys.token
updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys > saves the access token to localStorage under the TOKEN key
updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys > saves the refresh token under the REFRESHTOKEN key
updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys > saves the login time under the LOGGEDINTIME key
generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX > output of generateCardNumber preserves first and last 4 digits
generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX > correctly masks middle digits with X characters

skipped_files: test_repository/cross-dependent/auth-storage.cross.test.js  → validateEmailOrUsername (4 tests) — sources: signInFormHook.ts + constants.ts only, utilities.js NOT listed
test_repository/cross-dependent/payment-state.cross.test.js (sources: paymentReducer.js, actions.js, storage.ts, constants.ts — 12 tests SKIP)

coverage_gaps.detail: Explicit null/undefined guard added. Two new behaviours exist that have no test:
new_behaviours: checkWhiteSpace(null)      → returns false (previously: ternary coerced null to '' → also false, so behaviour unchanged)
checkWhiteSpace(undefined) → returns false (previously: ternary coerced undefined to '' → also false, so behaviour unchanged)
coverage_gaps.severity: LOW — behaviour did not actually change for null/undefined (ternary fallback was already handling it). The change is purely defensive. However, explicit tests would document the contract.
recommended_tests: checkWhiteSpace > returns false for a null input without throwing
checkWhiteSpace > returns false for an undefined input without throwing
checkWhiteSpace > returns false for an empty string

selection_proof.wait_validateEmail_note: 4 validateEmailOrUsername tests in auth-storage.cross.test.js are NOT selected — their sources only list signInFormHook.ts and constants.ts, NOT utilities.js. This is intentional: even though signInFormHook calls validateEmailOrUsername which might internally call capitalizeFirstLetter, the manifest only captures direct dependencies.
selection_proof.key_point: 3-line null guard → 17/77 tests selected. Tight, surgical selection that still catches the direct callers of checkWhiteSpace.

jest_run_command: npx jest --config test_repository/jest.config.js --no-coverage -t "checkWhiteSpace|capitalizeFirstLetter with checkWhiteSpace|checkNull|checkArray|getTimes|getProgressWidth|isUserLoggedIn reads storageKeys\.token|updateNewTokenDetails stores values under storageKeys|generateCardNumber output satisfies CARD_REGEX" --verbose
